laws.title: Public Law 15-99 | Second National Federal Torts Act
laws.status_basis: Source intact and fully operative. Its sole repeal is of PL 2-14 (the first National Federal Torts Act), and it establishes an operative federal tort regime. Nothing in the supplied set repeals PL 15-99.

laws.title: Public Law 15-100 | Employment Rights and Limitations Act
laws.status_basis: Source intact and fully operative; establishes employment limitations, protected leave, holidays, working hours, termination-notice rights, anti-discrimination, and enforcement. laws.title: Public Law 17-124 | Law Enforcement Accountability Act (LEAA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. A free-standing, generally-applicable regulatory statute (use-of-force, canine, and de-escalation rules for law enforcement). No self-repeal or sunset; SEC2 anti-supersession clause. It amends no numbered USAR Code section by express strike/insert.

laws.title: Public Law 17-125 | We Don't Read Minds Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Free-standing directive requiring each executive department's Inspector General to submit monthly status reports to Congress; penalty for noncompliance. No self-repeal or sunset. Listed under 'Repealed Laws' but body shows no repeal of itself.

laws.title: Public Law 17-126 | Special Events Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Establishes special-event permitting authority in the Department of Commerce and Labor for the District of Columbia. Local/District-focused regulatory scheme; no self-repeal or sunset.

laws.title: Public Law 17-127 | Border Security Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Sweeping amendatory immigration statute: SEC5 repeals all of Title 8 in its entirety, then SEC6-SEC13 rewrite numerous 8 U.S. Code sections with express 'shall now read as' replacement text. Numerous determinable textual amendments. No self-repeal/sunset. (Listed 'Repealed Laws' but source contains no self-repeal; PL-19-147 later repeals 8 USC 1102, a downstream change to be sequenced.)

laws.title: Public Law 17-128 | Impeachment Procedures and Protocols Act (IPPA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Congressional-procedure statute establishing impeachment inquiry/trial rules; SEC9 makes a determinable textual amendment to 28 U.S. Code 1365 (Congressional actions). No self-repeal; SEC2 anti-supersession. ('sunset' keyword in manifest is a false positive; no sunset clause exists.)

laws.title: Public Law 17-129 | Department of Education Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present (DOCX-extracted). Organic act establishing the Department of Education as an executive department; findings, purposes, definitions, officers, transfers, administrative provisions. Free-standing permanent general law; no self-repeal/sunset.

laws.title: Public Law 17-130 | Oath and Offices Protection (OOP)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Free-standing statute requiring officials to take the 5 U.S. Code 3331 oath within set timeframes, with forfeiture for failure; references 3331 as an existing anchor but does not restate/replace its text.

laws.title: Public Law 17-131 | More Jobs Amendment
laws.status_basis: Full source present (DOCX). An amendment to a prior USAR public law (PL 15-100, 'Employment Rights and Limitations Act'), not to a numbered U.S. Code section. Amends specified subsections of PL 15-100 with determinable replacement text. No self-repeal.

laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. The act repeals Public Law 26-190 section 108(c) and replaces it with a new temporary-assignment framework for former judges and justices. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

laws.title: Public Law 34-240 | Literally Fixing Taxes
laws.status_basis: The source text is complete and reviewable, but the archive classifies this law as a repealed-recorded historical enactment. It is a later amendment to PL 32-233's tax language, and the current XML note treats it as retained solely for statutory history.

laws.title: Public Law 34-241 | Protecting Working Americans
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3 directly revises the pay scale established in the prior pay-standardization law and should be applied as a textual amendment to the existing pay section. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

laws.title: Public Law 34-242 | Federal Courthouse Security & Jurisdiction Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. The act is a local/facilities-specific jurisdiction and security measure with no express positive-law Code amendment, so it is best preserved as statutory-note material rather than permanent section text. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

laws.title: Public Law 34-243 | Judicial Integrity & Accountability Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3 repeals 28 U.S.C. §§ 353, 359, and 361 and replaces chapter 16 part I with a new Supreme Court complaints-and-discipline framework. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

laws.title: Public Law 34-244 | Sentencing Reform Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3 amends Pub. L. No. 29-207 §§ 7(b) and 7(c), and Section 4 amends 18 U.S.C. § 3585. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

laws.title: Public Law 34-245 | Legal Compensation Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3 amends 18 U.S.C. § 3006A(d), and Section 4 repeals 42 U.S.C. chapter 34 subchapter X while creating a new grant-support framework. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

laws.title: Public Law 34-246 | Sentencing Commission Reform Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3(a) repeals 28 U.S.C. §§ 992, 994(p-s,w-x), and 995(a)(2-3); Section 3(b) rewrites § 991(a); and Section 3(c) adds new approval and responsibilities text to § 994. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

laws.title: Public Law 34-247 | Grand Jury Bypass Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. It creates a generally applicable administrative-subpoena and nondisclosure framework but does not supply a positive-law U.S. Code rewrite target, so it belongs in statutory-note form at the closest subject-matter anchor.

laws.title: Public Law 34-248 | Intelligence Community Inspector General Consolidation Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. It consolidates Intelligence Community Inspector General functions and adjusts oversight rules, but the enactment is best preserved as a statutory note anchored to Title 50 rather than as a clean positive-law rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 34-249 | Washington Diplomacy Complex Bill 2025
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. It is a place-specific diplomacy/security enactment with no express positive-law Code amendment, so it belongs in statutory-note form at the closest Title 40 anchor.

laws.title: Public Law 34-250 | Federal Prison Standards Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. It creates broad detention-facility standards, property-boundary rules, force rules, oversight, and remedies, but it does not supply a clean positive-law Code rewrite target. It belongs in statutory-note form at the closest Title 18 anchor.

laws.title: Public Law 34-251 | In-Game Sentencing Guidelines Bill
laws.status_basis: The source text is complete and reviewable, but the archive classifies this law as superseded history. It should remain retained only as statutory history rather than live positive text.

laws.title: Public Law 35-252 | Magistrate Appointment Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3 amends Section 106(c)(i) of Public Law 26-190, but it does not identify a clean positive-law U.S. Code text to rewrite. The enactment should remain note material at the closest Title 28 anchor.

laws.title: Public Law 35-253 | Nobody Likes Plex (Fiscal Year 25-4) Bill 2025
laws.status_basis: The source is an appropriations act with a short title, budget availability rule, line-item funding, an uncodified office designation, and an immediate effective date. Nothing in the text repeals the enactment or shows it to be superseded.

laws.title: Public Law 35-254 | An Act to repeal Public Law 28-205
laws.status_basis: The source is an operative repealer: it gives a short title, includes findings, and expressly states that Public Law 28-205 "is hereby repealed." No later enactment in the materials reviewed repeals this repealer.

laws.title: Public Law 35-255 | Print More Money Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The source is a live appropriations-style enactment: it authorizes issuance of fifty million dollars, sets spending limits and a reserved emergency fund, and contains an immediate effective date. Nothing in the text indicates repeal or expiration.

laws.title: Public Law 35-256 | Workers First Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The source reduces the income tax rate from 28 percent to 23 percent and then supplies supporting limitations and an immediate effective date. The text is operative, but it does not name a determinable U.S. Code target in the accessible source.

laws.title: Public Law 36-256 | Commerce Dispersal Act
laws.status_basis: The source dissolves the Department of Commerce and Labor, redistributes its functions, and transfers copyright administration. It is an operative structural reorganization, not a repealed enactment.

laws.title: Public Law 36-257 | Maritime Operations & Policy Overhaul Act
laws.status_basis: The source amends prior public laws governing military and Coast Guard policy, creates contingent Coast Guard authorities, and adds maritime-security coordination. It is operative and not shown as repealed.

laws.title: Public Law 36-258 | National Root Beer Day Act
laws.status_basis: The source is an active amendment to title 5: it inserts National Root Beer Day into the federal holiday list and includes an immediate enactment clause. No repeal or supersession appears in the materials reviewed.

laws.title: Public Law 36-259 | Run Me My Money Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The source is a supplemental appropriations bill that allocates money to specific departments and agencies, reserves an emergency fund, and takes effect immediately. It is still operative law.

laws.title: Public Law 36-260 | Fixing the District of Columbia Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The source expressly repeals six prior public laws, dissolves the District municipal government, transfers MPD and DCFEMS, reestablishes the D.C. Delegate, adopts municipal laws into federal law, and amends Public Law 22-156. Nothing in the materials shows it to be repealed or superseded.

laws.title: Public Law 37-261 | Federal Criminal Code Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The accessible source gives a short title, severability, an immediate effective date, and a section adopting an attached Federal Criminal Code. The act is operative, but the attachment text is not determinable from the accessible source alone.

laws.title: Public Law 38-262 | Fund the Government Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: Full text is present. The act is not self-repealing and includes both temporary appropriations and a permanent repeal/rewrite of prior schedule rules, so it remains operative rather than expired.

laws.title: Public Law 38-263 | National Security Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act creates an ongoing national-security eligibility framework, classification rules, and administrative-subpoena safeguards; it also repeals prior CRACK Act titles I-IV, but nothing in the source self-expires.

laws.title: Public Law 38-264 | Organized Crime and Racketeering Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act repeals CRACK Act titles V and VI and installs a continuing racketeering framework with penalties, civil remedies, and disqualification rules; it is not self-limiting or expired.

laws.title: Public Law 38-265 | Employment Clarity and Federal Workforce Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act expressly repeals earlier employment statutes and installs a continuing private- and federal-employment framework with review, anti-retaliation, and enforcement rules.

laws.title: Public Law 38-266 | Speedy Trial Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The source creates continuing speedy-trial rules and directly amends Public Law 4-32; nothing in the enactment self-terminates.

laws.title: Public Law 39-267 | Reform Sentencing Act
laws.status_basis: The source repeals PL 34-251 and replaces it with ongoing sentencing rules, enforcement, and multi-charge limits; it is not self-limiting or sunsetted.

laws.title: Public Law 40-268 | Big Beautiful Budget Act
laws.status_basis: This is a one-off appropriations act with short-form enactment language and no permanent statutory target; the appropriations and oversight provisions are time-bound rather than enduring Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 41-269 | Federal Enhancement Against Foreign Intrusion Amendments Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act makes continuing amendments to Public Law 24-173 and adds a new disclosure/enforcement section; nothing in the source sunsets or self-repeals.

laws.title: Public Law 41-270 | Washington Diplomacy Complex Corrections Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act directly revises the Washington Diplomacy Complex Act 2025 in multiple sections and adds technical corrections; it is an ongoing corrective amendment, not a repealed or expired law.

laws.title: Public Law 41-271 | The Great Change Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: This is a broad integrating act that repeals, amends, transfers, and preserves multiple existing laws while installing continuing reforms across security, commerce, ethics, military service, and whistleblower enforcement.
